meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills: Debugging for Students (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills to crush debugging blockers. A high-impact case study for students using Antigravity and Claude to accelerate your development.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging gridlock, integrate Claude Code into your terminal workflow. By combining Antigravity’s architectural insights with Claude’s iterative execution, you transform opaque error logs into actionable patches. This setup allows you to identify root causes, simulate fixes, and maintain code quality through automated, context-aware terminal interactions.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal overflowing with red text, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat you can’t stabilize. You’ve refactored, you’ve googled, and you’ve prayed to the stack-overflow gods, but the bug remains—a silent phantom in your codebase. This is the 'debugging wall,' the single point where most student projects wither and die.

But here is the secret: professional software engineering isn't about knowing every syntax rule; it's about mastering the feedback loop. By leveraging Claude Code alongside Antigravity, you stop guessing and start engineering. This isn't just about fixing a line of code; it's about upgrading your cognitive toolkit to solve problems at a senior velocity.

steps.title: Environment Context Injection
steps.description: Before debugging, you must ground your AI in your current directory structure and dependency map to prevent hallucinatory fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory and generate a high-level dependency graph of the problematic module.

steps.title: Isolating the Failure Surface
steps.description: Use Claude to trace the execution path starting from the error log back to the entry point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given this error log, trace the execution flow and identify the specific line where the state becomes inconsistent.

steps.title: Simulating the Fix
steps.description: Never apply a fix directly to production code. Use a sandbox approach to verify the logic first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a patch for this logic error that follows DRY principles and doesn't break existing interfaces.

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure your fix doesn't introduce new bugs by running local test suites through the Claude terminal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test for the newly patched logic to ensure edge cases are covered.

steps.title: Final Commit and Documentation
steps.description: Document your fix so you can explain the 'why' behind the code during interviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional commit message and a brief explanation of the bug fix for my portfolio documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'fix: resolve race condition in authentication middleware'

pro_tips: Always set your Claude system prompt to 'Senior Engineer' to force more robust, production-grade code suggestions.
Use Antigravity to generate 'Architecture Decision Records' (ADRs) for every major fix to boost your portfolio impact.
If Claude gets stuck in a loop, force a context reset by clearing the current chat session and re-feeding the error log.
Leverage the CLI history to identify recurring bug patterns in your code—this is your most valuable data for improvement.
Pipe your terminal output directly into Claude using command redirection to save time on manual copy-pasting.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, attempt to build a simple CLI tool that automates one aspect of your workflow using these same Claude Code commands.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same debugging workflow used by top-tier engineering firms. Don't let bugs define your progress—let your tools do the heavy lifting. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
